query: Our customer-support agent uses the Anthropic API with a roughly 5,000-token system prompt (persona + tool definitions + 4,000 tokens of few-shot examples that never change) plus a ~200-token user message on every turn. We send about 50,000 conversations per day, average 8 turns per conversation, and conversations are spread across days. Per-call cost has been climbing. Audit the prompt for budget and caching, and tell me where to put cache_control and which TTL to use.
expected_behavior: Identifies that the 5,000-token system block (instructions + tool defs + few-shot examples) is the stable prefix that should be cached, and recommends placing cache_control at the END of the system content (not in the user message or the volatile region)

Recommends the explicit '1h' (extended) TTL — not the default 5m — and justifies it via the multi-day cross-conversation reuse pattern (or equivalently warns that the default reverted to 5m and must be set explicitly to get extended caching)

Projects a meaningful cost / savings outcome (either a percentage savings on input cost, or a write-cost-vs-read-cost comparison, or a break-even hit-rate analysis) to demonstrate that the cache pays back at the user's call volume
